  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "sg-evals/TypeScript--v5.7.2", "path": "src/compiler/moduleNameResolver.ts", "symbol": "resolveModuleName"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/TypeScript--v5.7.2", "path": "src/compiler/moduleNameResolver.ts", "symbol": "node16ModuleNameResolver"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/TypeScript--v5.7.2", "path": "src/compiler/moduleNameResolver.ts", "symbol": "nodeNextModuleNameResolverWorker"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/TypeScript--v5.7.2", "path": "src/compiler/moduleNameResolver.ts", "symbol": "loadModuleFromExports"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/TypeScript--v5.7.2", "path": "src/compiler/moduleNameResolver.ts", "symbol": "classicNameResolver"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/TypeScript--v5.7.2", "path": "src/compiler/moduleNameResolver.ts", "symbol": "getPackageJsonInfo"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/TypeScript--v5.7.2", "path": "src/compiler/moduleNameResolver.ts", "symbol": "tryLoadModuleUsingPaths"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/TypeScript--v5.7.2", "path": "src/compiler/utilities.ts", "symbol": "matchPatternOrExact"}
  ],
  "text": "All module resolution logic for TypeScript v5.7.2 is concentrated in src/compiler/moduleNameResolver.ts.\n\n**Entry Point & Strategy Dispatch:**\n`resolveModuleName` (line ~1401) is the main entry point. It reads `compilerOptions.moduleResolution` (defaulting via `getEmitModuleResolutionKind`), traces the selected strategy, and dispatches to:\n- `node16ModuleNameResolver` for `ModuleResolutionKind.Node16`\n- `nodeNextModuleNameResolver` for `ModuleResolutionKind.NodeNext`\n- `bundlerModuleNameResolver` for `ModuleResolutionKind.Bundler`\n- `classicNameResolver` for `ModuleResolutionKind.Classic`\n- (Node/Node10 handled inline or via a separate nodeModuleNameResolver)\n\n**Node16 Resolver:**\n`node16ModuleNameResolver` (line ~1704) delegates to `nodeNextModuleNameResolverWorker` with `NodeResolutionFeatures.Node16Default`. The worker handles ESM/CJS mode-aware resolution including package.json `exports` field via `loadModuleFromExports` (line ~2613) and `loadModuleFromExportsOrImports` (line ~2702).\n\n**Classic Resolver:**\n`classicNameResolver` (line ~3250) implements the legacy TypeScript Classic resolution strategy (walking up directories without node_modules lookups).\n\n**Bundler Resolver:**\n`bundlerModuleNameResolver` (line ~1758) implements the Bundler strategy.\n\n**package.json Helper:**\n`getPackageJsonInfo` (line ~2440) reads and caches package.json data from disk. The cache interface `PackageJsonInfoCache` (line ~929) and `createPackageJsonInfoCache` (line ~1046) manage in-memory caching of package.json info keyed by path.\n\n**Path Pattern Matching:**\nPath mapping (`paths` compiler option) is handled by `tryLoadModuleUsingPaths` (line ~3158) and `tryLoadModuleUsingPathsIfEligible` (line ~1562), which call `matchPatternOrExact` from `src/compiler/utilities.ts` (line ~10200) to match module names against path patterns.\n\n**Tracing/Diagnostic Reporting:**\n`isTraceEnabled` (line ~120) checks if resolution tracing is active. Throughout `resolveModuleName` and all strategy workers, `trace(host, Diagnostics.*)` calls emit diagnostic messages for resolution steps and failures (e.g., `Diagnostics.Module_resolution_kind_is_not_specified_using_0`, `Diagnostics.Module_resolution_using_rootDirs_has_failed`).\n\n**Supporting Files:**\n- `src/compiler/resolutionCache.ts` — caches resolution results across compilations, wraps `resolveModuleName`\n- `src/compiler/program.ts` — invokes `resolveModuleName` during program creation\n- `src/compiler/moduleSpecifiers.ts` — uses `getPackageJsonInfo` and `matchPatternOrExact` for module specifier generation\n- `src/compiler/utilities.ts` — provides `matchPatternOrExact` for path pattern matching"
}

  "text": "All module resolution logic for TypeScript v5.7.2 is concentrated in src/compiler/moduleNameResolver.ts.\n\n**Entry Point & Strategy Dispatch:**\n`resolveModuleName` (line ~1401) is the main entry point. It reads `compilerOptions.moduleResolution` (defaulting via `getEmitModuleResolutionKind`), traces the selected strategy, and dispatches to:\n- `node16ModuleNameResolver` for `ModuleResolutionKind.Node16`\n- `nodeNextModuleNameResolver` for `ModuleResolutionKind.NodeNext`\n- `bundlerModuleNameResolver` for `ModuleResolutionKind.Bundler`\n- `classicNameResolver` for `ModuleResolutionKind.Classic`\n\n**Node16 Resolver:**\n`node16ModuleNameResolver` (line ~1704) delegates to `nodeNextModuleNameResolverWorker` with `NodeResolutionFeatures.Node16Default`. The worker handles ESM/CJS mode-aware resolution including package.json `exports` field via `loadModuleFromExports` (line ~2613) and `loadModuleFromExportsOrImports` (line ~2702).\n\n**Classic Resolver:**\n`classicNameResolver` (line ~3250) implements the legacy TypeScript Classic resolution strategy (walking up directories without node_modules lookups).\n\n**Bundler Resolver:**\n`bundlerModuleNameResolver` (line ~1758) implements the Bundler strategy.\n\n**package.json Helper:**\n`getPackageJsonInfo` (line ~2440) reads and caches package.json data from disk. The cache interface `PackageJsonInfoCache` (line ~929) and `createPackageJsonInfoCache` (line ~1046) manage in-memory caching of package.json info keyed by path.\n\n**Path Pattern Matching:**\nPath mapping (`paths` compiler option) is handled by `tryLoadModuleUsingPaths` (line ~3158) and `tryLoadModuleUsingPathsIfEligible` (line ~1562), which call `matchPatternOrExact` from `src/compiler/utilities.ts` (line ~10200) to match module names against path patterns.\n\n**Tracing/Diagnostic Reporting:**\n`isTraceEnabled` (line ~120) checks if resolution tracing is active. Throughout `resolveModuleName` and all strategy workers, `trace(host, Diagnostics.*)` calls emit diagnostic messages for resolution steps and failures (e.g., `Diagnostics.Module_resolution_kind_is_not_specified_using_0`, `Diagnostics.Module_resolution_using_rootDirs_has_failed`).\n\n**Supporting Files:**\n- `src/compiler/resolutionCache.ts` - caches resolution results across compilations, wraps `resolveModuleName`\n- `src/compiler/program.ts` - invokes `resolveModuleName` during program creation\n- `src/compiler/moduleSpecifiers.ts` - uses `getPackageJsonInfo` and `matchPatternOrExact` for module specifier generation\n- `src/compiler/utilities.ts` - provides `matchPatternOrExact` for path pattern matching"

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/TypeScript--v5.7.2` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/TypeScript--v5.7.2$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

# TypeScript Module Resolution Migration from Classic to Node16

## Your Task

Find all TypeScript source files in microsoft/TypeScript under src/compiler/ that implement module resolution. Identify: the moduleNameResolver entry point and its strategy dispatch (Classic vs Node vs Node16 vs Bundler), the node16ModuleNameResolver and its package.json exports field resolution, the classicNameResolver, the getPackageJsonInfo helper, the pathPatternMatch for path mapping, and the tracing/diagnostic reporting for resolution failures. Report each file path and key function or class.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/TypeScript--v5.7.2.

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
Now I have enough information to write the answer. The key file is `src/compiler/moduleNameResolver.ts` which contains all the major functions. Let me also verify the tracing functions.
Now I have everything I need. Let me write the answer.

The answer has been written to `/workspace/answer.json`. Here's a summary of findings:

## Key Files in `src/compiler/`

### Primary File: `moduleNameResolver.ts`
Contains virtually all module resolution logic:

| Symbol | Role |
|--------|------|
| `resolveModuleName` | **Entry point** — dispatches to strategy based on `compilerOptions.moduleResolution` |
| `node16ModuleNameResolver` | Node16 strategy (delegates to `nodeNextModuleNameResolverWorker` with Node16 features) |
| `nodeNextModuleNameResolver` | NodeNext strategy |
| `nodeNextModuleNameResolverWorker` | Shared worker for Node16/NodeNext with ESM/CJS mode awareness |
| `bundlerModuleNameResolver` | Bundler strategy |
| `classicNameResolver` | Classic strategy (legacy TS resolution) |
| `loadModuleFromExports` | Resolves `package.json` `exports` field |
| `loadModuleFromExportsOrImports` | Handles both `exports` and `imports` fields |
| `getPackageJsonInfo` | Reads/caches `package.json` from disk |
| `PackageJsonInfoCache` / `createPackageJsonInfoCache` | Cache interface and implementation |
| `tryLoadModuleUsingPaths` | Path mapping (`paths` tsconfig option) |
| `isTraceEnabled` | Checks if `traceResolution` is active; used throughout for diagnostic output |

### Supporting Files
- **`src/compiler/utilities.ts`** — `matchPatternOrExact`: matches module names against path patterns (used by `tryLoadModuleUsingPaths`)
- **`src/compiler/resolutionCache.ts`** — wraps and caches `resolveModuleName` results across incremental compilations
- **`src/compiler/program.ts`** — calls `resolveModuleName` during program creation
- **`src/compiler/moduleSpecifiers.ts`** — uses `getPackageJsonInfo` and `matchPatternOrExact` for generating module specifiers

  "text": "All module resolution logic for TypeScript v5.7.2 is concentrated in src/compiler/moduleNameResolver.ts.\n\n**Entry Point & Strategy Dispatch:**\n`resolveModuleName` (line ~1401) is the main entry point. It reads `compilerOptions.moduleResolution` (defaulting via `getEmitModuleResolutionKind`), traces the selected strategy, and dispatches to:\n- `node16ModuleNameResolver` for `ModuleResolutionKind.Node16`\n- `nodeNextModuleNameResolver` for `ModuleResolutionKind.NodeNext`\n- `bundlerModuleNameResolver` for `ModuleResolutionKind.Bundler`\n- `classicNameResolver` for `ModuleResolutionKind.Classic`\n- (Node/Node10 handled inline or via a separate nodeModuleNameResolver)\n\n**Node16 Resolver:**\n`node16ModuleNameResolver` (line ~1704) delegates to `nodeNextModuleNameResolverWorker` with `NodeResolutionFeatures.Node16Default`. The worker handles ESM/CJS mode-aware resolution including package.json `exports` field via `loadModuleFromExports` (line ~2613) and `loadModuleFromExportsOrImports` (line ~2702).\n\n**Classic Resolver:**\n`classicNameResolver` (line ~3250) implements the legacy TypeScript Classic resolution strategy (walking up directories without node_modules lookups).\n\n**Bundler Resolver:**\n`bundlerModuleNameResolver` (line ~1758) implements the Bundler strategy.\n\n**package.json Helper:**\n`getPackageJsonInfo` (line ~2440) reads and caches package.json data from disk. The cache interface `PackageJsonInfoCache` (line ~929) and `createPackageJsonInfoCache` (line ~1046) manage in-memory caching of package.json info keyed by path.\n\n**Path Pattern Matching:**\nPath mapping (`paths` compiler option) is handled by `tryLoadModuleUsingPaths` (line ~3158) and `tryLoadModuleUsingPathsIfEligible` (line ~1562), which call `matchPatternOrExact` from `src/compiler/utilities.ts` (line ~10200) to match module names against path patterns.\n\n**Tracing/Diagnostic Reporting:**\n`isTraceEnabled` (line ~120) checks if resolution tracing is active. Throughout `resolveModuleName` and all strategy workers, `trace(host, Diagnostics.*)` calls emit diagnostic messages for resolution steps and failures (e.g., `Diagnostics.Module_resolution_kind_is_not_specified_using_0`, `Diagnostics.Module_resolution_using_rootDirs_has_failed`).\n\n**Supporting Files:**\n- `src/compiler/resolutionCache.ts` — caches resolution results across compilations, wraps `resolveModuleName`\n- `src/compiler/program.ts` — invokes `resolveModuleName` during program creation\n- `src/compiler/moduleSpecifiers.ts` — uses `getPackageJsonInfo` and `matchPatternOrExact` for module specifier generation\n- `src/compiler/utilities.ts` — provides `matchPatternOrExact` for path pattern matching"
}

The worker handles ESM/CJS mode-aware resolution including package.json `exports` field via `loadModuleFromExports` (line ~2613) and `loadModuleFromExportsOrImports` (line ~2702).\n\n**Classic Resolver:**\n`classicNameResolver` (line ~3250) implements the legacy TypeScript Classic resolution strategy (walking up directories without node_modules lookups).\n\n**Bundler Resolver:**\n`bundlerModuleNameResolver` (line ~1758) implements the Bundler strategy.\n\n**package.json Helper:**\n`getPackageJsonInfo` (line ~2440) reads and caches package.json data from disk. The cache interface `PackageJsonInfoCache` (line ~929) and `createPackageJsonInfoCache` (line ~1046) manage in-memory caching of package.json info keyed by path.\n\n**Path Pattern Matching:**\nPath mapping (`paths` compiler option) is handled by `tryLoadModuleUsingPaths` (line ~3158) and `tryLoadModuleUsingPathsIfEligible` (line ~1562), which call `matchPatternOrExact` from `src/compiler/utilities.ts` (line ~10200) to match module names against path patterns.\n\n**Tracing/Diagnostic Reporting:**\n`isTraceEnabled` (line ~120) checks if resolution tracing is active. Throughout `resolveModuleName` and all strategy workers, `trace(host, Diagnostics.*)` calls emit diagnostic messages for resolution steps and failures (e.g., `Diagnostics.Module_resolution_kind_is_not_specified_using_0`, `Diagnostics.Module_resolution_using_rootDirs_has_failed`).\n\n**Supporting Files:**\n- `src/compiler/resolutionCache.ts` - caches resolution results across compilations, wraps `resolveModuleName`\n- `src/compiler/program.ts` - invokes `resolveModuleName` during program creation\n- `src/compiler/moduleSpecifiers.ts` - uses `getPackageJsonInfo` and `matchPatternOrExact` for module specifier generation\n- `src/compiler/utilities.ts` - provides `matchPatternOrExact` for path pattern matching"
}
